questions.prompt: Why is testing restores quarterly important?

choices.text: Backups that haven't been verified might not work. Hidden corruption, schema mismatches, missing permissions, or restore-procedure bugs only surface during an actual restore. Teams that skip restore drills almost always discover bugs the day they really need it — exactly the worst time. A 15-minute quarterly drill (restore yesterday's backup to a new project, verify row counts and key tables, delete the test project) gives you confidence the process works and familiarity with the steps. The day you need a real restore, you'll know exactly what to do.

questions.explanation: Untested backups aren't backups. Practice quarterly so the procedure works under pressure.

questions.prompt: GDPR compliance — user requests their data deleted. Schema readiness?

choices.text: Set up cascade deletes from `auth.users` throughout the schema: every user-owned table references `auth.users(id)` with `on delete cascade`. Then deletion is one call: `supabaseAdmin.auth.admin.deleteUser(userId)` — the cascade graph removes profiles, posts, comments, settings, audit log entries, all linked data. Test this BEFORE the first request comes in: create a test user, populate associated rows, delete the user, verify all linked rows are gone. Discovering missing cascades during an enforcement deadline is a fire drill.

questions.explanation: Cascade FKs from auth.users + one-call deletion. Test before you need it.
